context.problem_statement: Ad-hoc exploration of complex systems lacks rigor, reproducibility, and coverage guarantees. Users need a methodology that applies implementation-level rigor to exploration tasks— ensuring nothing is missed, findings are evidence-based, and conclusions are cross-validated.
context.existing_landscape: - Explore agent: Single perspective, no coverage tracking, no cross-validation - feature-dev:code-explorer: Traces specific features, not comprehensive exploration - deep-analysis: Decision analysis framework, not system exploration - Gap: No skill provides rigorous, multi-perspective, evidence-based exploration methodology
context.target_audience: Developers and analysts who need to thoroughly understand complex systems (codebases, architectures, documentation sets) before making decisions or changes.

requirements.explicit: - Rigorous exploration methodology - Comprehensive coverage of complex systems - Generalized scope (any complex system, not just codebases) - Framework for Rigor as separate reference file - Multi-agent deployment with cross-validation - Evidence-based findings with provenance - Coverage matrix tracking
requirements.implicit: - Integration with Claude Code tools (Task, Grep, Glob, Read) - Parallel agent deployment (single message, multiple Task calls) - Reproducible methodology (documented process) - Actionable deliverables - Handles emergent scope (unknown unknowns)
requirements.discovered: - Calibration modes needed (light/medium/deep) to match rigor to stakes - Conflict resolution protocol for agent disagreements - Stopping criteria to prevent infinite exploration - Graceful degradation if agents fail - Audit trail for all findings - Domain adaptation templates

architecture.pattern_rationale: Exploration requires ordered phases (pre-flight before agents, synthesis after), but agent work is parallelizable. Hybrid pattern gives both structure and efficiency.

phase.name: Pre-Flight
phase.purpose: Gather existing knowledge before starting exploration
phase.actions: - Review prior context (project docs, git history, user-provided info) - Review recent changes (git log, changelogs) - Define scope with rationale - Set calibration level (light/medium/deep)

phase.name: Parallel Agent Deployment

agents.agent: Enumerate all components, build complete map, count everything
Identify conventions, assess consistency, find architectural patterns
Verify accuracy, recover intent, find documentation gaps
Find absent components, broken references, stale content, opportunities

phase.verification: All agents complete, coverage matrix updated

phase.name: Cross-Validation
phase.actions: - Compare component counts across agents - Identify conflicting findings - Resolve conflicts through targeted investigation - Update confidence levels

phase.name: Synthesis
phase.actions: - Merge findings into unified document - Fill coverage matrix - Rank opportunities by impact - Document methodology for reproducibility
phase.verification: Deliverable complete, all sections filled, methodology documented

decision.name: Calibration Level
decision.criteria: Match to stakes: how important is comprehensive understanding?

decision.name: Domain Adaptation
decision.criteria: What type of system is being explored?

files.file: Main skill file with methodology overview, quick start, phases, and verification
Foundational framework: three pillars, evidence requirements, completion protocol Generalized beyond exploration to any rigorous task
Detailed prompt templates for four perspective agents Domain-specific adaptations included
Coverage matrix templates for different domains Instructions for custom matrix creation

scripts.decision_summary: needs_scripts: false (v1.0) rationale: Skill is primarily methodology, not automation. Scripts add value but aren't essential. Focus v1.0 on methodology quality. Consider for v2.0: - coverage_matrix.py for state tracking - validate_exploration.py for deliverable verification

evolution_analysis.score_justification:
Core methodology based on timeless principles: - Triangulation (multiple perspectives) - research standard since 1970s - Evidence hierarchy - fundamental to epistemology - Coverage tracking - basic completeness requirement - Cross-validation - scientific method foundation
Agent prompts and domain templates may need updates, but framework endures.
evolution_analysis.extension_points: 1. Domain templates: Add new domains beyond codebase/documentation/architecture 2. Agent perspectives: Could add specialized agents (Security, Performance, etc.) 3. Scripts: Add automation for coverage tracking and validation 4. Integration: Chain with brainstorming (before) and planning (after) 5. Calibration modes: Could add more granular levels
evolution_analysis.obsolescence_triggers: - Claude gaining native comprehensive exploration capability (unlikely soon) - Fundamental change in how codebases are structured (unlikely) - New paradigm for understanding complex systems (would require adaptation, not obsolescence)

pattern.name: No Coverage Tracking
pattern.description: Exploring without tracking what's been explored
pattern.alternative: Coverage matrix with explicit status per cell

pattern.name: Assertions Without Evidence
pattern.alternative: Evidence requirements per finding (source, confidence, verification)

pattern.name: Infinite Exploration
pattern.alternative: Explicit completion criteria and calibration levels

pattern.name: Ignoring Prior Knowledge
pattern.description: Starting fresh without checking existing work
pattern.alternative: Pre-flight phase with project docs, git history, and user context

success_criteria.criterion: All cells in coverage matrix have status (explored, partial, N/A) Verification: Matrix review, no '?' remaining
Every finding cites source (file:line or specific location) Verification: Sample audit of 10 findings
Agent findings compared and conflicts resolved Verification: Conflict log shows resolution for each conflict
What was looked for but not found is recorded Verification: Negative findings section populated
Another person could follow the documented process Verification: Methodology section complete with queries, tools, order
All required sections filled, no placeholders Verification: Deliverable structure check
